item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The image that was used in this article is non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0) as it was a combination of this album cover and an image which could not be sourced, with no indication either was released under Creative Commons. Thus, the image has been removed.

item.title: Staff post
item.content:encoded: Noting that revisions 24 and 25 were made without authorial permission and alter the language of the article. Version has been reverted to 23.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Those of us old enough to remember the "satanic panic" of the 80's going hysteric over heavy metal also remember when records and tapes had two sides. And as (account deleted) points out, Suicide Solution was the last song on the first side. So jumping straight to track 6 would indeed have required a spatial anomaly.

Still, remembering all that insanity, this scip amuses me.

(To those saying the scip should have used a more obscure artist, it being this track was the point1) Though I do agree the black boxes are kind of silly, given that the cover art is shown and it's such a well known album.

So, have they found the comparably-anomalous Judas Priest2 disc yet?

Footnotes
1. unhinged fundamentalists were claiming that Suicide Solution encouraged people to commit suicide, despite the fact that a cursory glance at the lyrics would prove this false.
2. Priest were taken to court over alleged backwards messages — supposedly embedded in a cover song, no less — urging listeners to commit suicide. The judge, fortunately, was sane enough to see how much nonsense that was.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I know the Blizzard of Ozz LP and I also know that there are 5 tracks on the A-side and 4 on the B-side. So how could the needle skip from track 4 to 6?
Aside of that little minor thing, this was one of the best music SCPs I ever read.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't really care for the effect. It feels like a "crazy to death" SCiP minus the "death". It's just… crazy to catatonia. There are a lot of cool elements, but I'm underwhelmed.

-1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well, I guess if he used something more vague and indie like some obscure grunge band from seattle or something, it would work a lot better.

Kinda like SCP-99.
It was an unfamiliar painting by an obscure surreal painter.
It worked.

So, I'm suggesting some one hit wonder band from the seventies, or something similar instead of goddamn Ozzy Osbourne.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This article feels underated.
I kinda like it, but only because I'm in to the whole "subliminal messaging" thing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It has nothing to do with the legality of using the album, just that there's no point of partially redacting anything to anyone who was even vaguely aware of pop culture in the 1980s. I knew what it was immediately and I've never heard the album.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Agreed. Given that it's explicitly stated that other copies do not have this property, I don't see any point in redacting the title. Especially when, as icing on the "pointless" cake, you go ahead and show a picture of the cover!

But the biggest problem is that I'm really not seeing much here to distinguish it from existing variations on the theme of "recording what makes people commit suicide".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So basicly Ozzy ozbourne (don't care how to spell his name if it's wrong) made the devils music?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's author choice to redact. I didn't know what it was until I looked it up.

And it actually gives a tad bit of legal cover under fair-use/parody laws.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like the fact that this isn't an overt "oh nos this thing eats faces" SCP yet remains very unnerving, at least for me ("What if that track is actually some sort of escape and it we who are trapped in a shallow facsimile of reality that like a bunch of Sims?").

Solid upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Is there any point to redacting the name of the album when the cover art makes it obvious that it's a copy of Blizzard of Ozz, and that track 5 must therefore be Suicide Solution?

I say either use just an unlabelled disc for the article picture, or unredact it and explicitly state it's an Ozzy album.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Eh…

I'm not really a fan of random redacted/censoring when it doesn't seem like the information would actually be problematic. Having so much of it, especially since this track seems to be the only one of its kind affected, really ends up breaking it for me as far as readability.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It was very immersive, but a tad disappointing. I don't know what I was expecting, but the ending left me a bit underwhelmed. It's pretty cool how the subjects start observing things in a higher dimension, but "stuck in the fantasy" seems a bit like a cheat ending to an otherwise very cool SCP. Maybe consider the individual physically interacting with the dimension, or being pulled in all together?

Whatever, upvoted

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm surprised, yet not surprised it's an ozzy album.

However, I'm sure you could use Stairway to heaven by Led Zeppelin as well with all the rumors surrounding it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yep, and the choice of album was no accident.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: http://www.amazon.com/Blizzard-Ozz-Ozzy-Osbourne/dp/B000063DFT

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
okay, the effect could be more interesting , but oh hell, the image. Just where did you get that?
upvoted - any half decent SCP with that would get it, really.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it overall- it's not overt and it's well put together, but one thing strikes me as (for lack of a better word) unnecessary: the nonstandard topography. There's a nice parallel between the abnormal dimensionality of the hallucinations and that of the track, but in the end it feels like… a V8 in a lawnmower. Like the same thing could be accomplished far more subtly with mechanical means. Higher dimensions (or things involving them) are generally SCPs in and of themselves.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I added a line about not knowing the language. Given the fact that it's "similar" to track five played backwards, we can assume that its melodically and rhythmically similar.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: How exactly are the lyrics on the anomalous track "different"? Are they similar to the original, or completely different in tone and/or subject matter?

item.title: Re: Added some more effect...
item.content:encoded:
And now there's a part of my brain wondering if the lyrics to Track 5 include the words, "Ph'nglui mglw'nafh Cthulhu R'lyeh wgah'nagl fhtagn…" :)

(NO, for the love of the Old Ones, DON'T put that in - I just have a weird sense of humor.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I saw the original and I like what you did to it also.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I didn't see the original, but whatever you did to it I like it. Upvoted.

item.title: Added some more effect...
item.content:encoded: There's now a reason for turning suicidal after listening to this thing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have to agree with Severe here - good buildup, disappointing payoff. I'm withholding my vote for the nonce, because I think you can make this into something really, really good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As I read this, my thought process was as follows "Mhmm…okay, solid…..oh wow, mobius strip, that's pretty cool…..oh man, this is getting really good, I can't wait to see what it actually does!……wait, that's it?"

In short, you have a great build up that lead me up to…not a whole lot. You had a really cool idea with the 5th track being basically hidden, but then when you actually get to what the hidden track actually does, it completely fizzles out. Plus:

6. Things what make you go nuts for no apparent reason.

Making you suicidal is just a more boring version of this item on the list.

So yeah, solid idea that starts off really well, then fizzles out to nothing. Downvoted for now, but if you can work on the ending (maybe talk to some people in chat for help if you are stumped), I can see this actually being a great SCP.

EDIT: Nice improvement. Still not as good as I think it could be, but good enough to deserve an upvote. Well done.

item.title: My attempt at a contest entry
item.content:encoded: If anyone remembers people freaking out in the eighties over heavy-metal music and the subliminal satanic messages within, this is for you :)
